The subject of praise is the blessings of grace flowing from God’s everlasting love. The name of God as a covenant God and Father in Christ, blessing us with all spiritual blessings in him, should be loved and praised. The Lord chose a people for himself so that they might be to him for a name and praise. If they do not praise him for this special favor, then they are the most unworthy and ungrateful of all people.

God is, and will always be, the same to his church: a gracious, faithful, wonder-working God. And his church is, and will be, the same to him: a thankful, praising people; thus his name endures forever. He will return in ways of mercy to them, and he will delight to do them good.

These verses protect believers against idolatry and all false worship by showing what kind of gods the Gentiles worshipped. And the more deplorable the condition of the Gentile nations that worship idols, the more we should be thankful that we know better. Let us pity, pray for, and seek to benefit those who are in darkness and those who are deceived. Let us strive to glorify his name and recommend his truth, not only with our words but by holy lives, copying the example of Christ’s goodness and truth.
